description:
## Goal

Segment raw texts into citation-addressable chunks (200-800 tokens) that respect each document's natural structure. Every chunk carries enough metadata to produce a full `[Tradition | Text | Section]` citation.

## Architecture

Per-text chunking configs in `chunking/{tradition}/{text_id}.toml` declare a strategy (regex / heading / paragraph-grouping), a section pattern, and a `section_label_format`. `chunk.py` reads the raw file, dispatches to the matching splitter, runs each chunk through a token counter (tiktoken), and writes one TOML per chunk to `corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/chunks/`. Chunks that exceed 800 tokens are split at the nearest paragraph boundary with a sub-section suffix on the chunk ID (e.g. `V-2-1a`, `V-2-1b`).

## Outputs

- `chunking/{tradition}/{text_id}.toml` — per-text chunking rules
- `corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/chunks/*.toml` — addressable chunks
- `corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/metadata.toml` — text-level metadata
- `corpus/traditions.toml` — registry derived from chunking configs

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every raw text produces at least one chunk
- No chunk exceeds 800 tokens; no chunk is split mid-sentence
- Every chunk has a `section` field matching the text's native division system
- Chunk IDs are unique across the entire corpus
- Round-trip: concatenating all chunks for a text reconstructs the original (modulo whitespace normalization)

## See Also

docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 2: Chunking"
